presenceCondition:
(or (and CONFIG_PCI
         (not __need___FILE)
         __STRICT_ANSI__
         __USE_EXTERN_INLINES
         CONFIG_PCI)
    (and CONFIG_PCI (not __need___FILE) (not __USE_EXTERN_INLINES) CONFIG_PCI)
    (and CONFIG_PCI
         (not __need___FILE)
         (not __STRICT_ANSI__)
         __USE_EXTERN_INLINES
         CONFIG_PCI))
model:
(define-fun CONFIG_PCI () Bool
  true)
(define-fun __STRICT_ANSI__ () Bool
  true)
(define-fun __USE_EXTERN_INLINES () Bool
  true)
(define-fun __need___FILE () Bool
  false)
reasoning: 76baeebf7df.c L137: `printf("mask: %ld\n", *mask)` — null dereference of mask. mask is set from cpumask_of_node which in the !CONFIG_DEBUG_PER_CPU_MAPS variant (L51-53) returns node_to_cpumask_map[node] directly — this can be NULL if the node has not been set up. The null deref requires CONFIG_NUMA=T (so node_to_cpumask_map is used) and !CONFIG_DEBUG_PER_CPU_MAPS (so the non-checking variant is used) and CONFIG_PCI=T (so local_cpus_show is reachable). New bug (no ERROR). PC simplifies to CONFIG_PCI=T AND !__need___FILE (the `!__need___FILE` is always true in normal compilation, so effectively CONFIG_PCI=T). PC is under-constrained — missing CONFIG_NUMA=T and !CONFIG_DEBUG_PER_CPU_MAPS requirements. Without CONFIG_NUMA=T, cpumask_of_node always returns &cpu_online_mask (never NULL).
